lecture_context.lecture_title: Lecture 17 - ConvNets and TreeRNNs for NLP

unit.name: Recursive neural tensor networks and phrase-level supervision
unit.description: Introduces the recursive neural tensor network and the Stanford Sentiment Treebank used to train it.
unit.summary: To address the rigidity of a single shared composition matrix, the lecture introduces the recursive neural tensor network (RNTN), which models richer interactions between phrase components via multiplicative tensor-based composition [ts=3302s][ts=3344s]. The motivation is semantic asymmetry: adjective-noun combinations such as 'red ball' and verb-object combinations such as 'kick the ball' require different kinds of composition [ts=3316s][ts=3336s]. The tensor layer is described as learning multiple intermediate matrices that together form a tensor, enabling vector-tensor-vector interactions instead of only a linear transform over concatenated children [ts=3351s][ts=3388s]. The model is then tied to sentiment analysis through the Stanford Sentiment Treebank, where nearly 12,000 parsed sentences receive sentiment labels not just at the sentence level but on every phrase node in the tree [ts=3419s][ts=3595s]. Phrase-level supervision itself improves simpler baselines from 79% to 83%, and the RNTN further improves over bigram Naive Bayes by roughly 2.5%, showing that richer composition plus denser supervision can help [ts=3718s][ts=3785s].

key_points.text: RNTNs were introduced to model different kinds of composition more flexibly than a single shared matrix allows.
Adjective-noun and verb-object combinations require different semantic interactions.
The neural tensor layer replaces a purely linear child-composition step with vector-tensor-vector interactions.
The Stanford Sentiment Treebank labels every phrase node, not just whole sentences, with sentiment.
Phrase-level supervision boosts even simple baselines, and the RNTN improves further over bigram Naive Bayes.

kp_catalog.name: Recursive neural tensor network
kp_catalog.description: An RNTN uses tensor-mediated interactions to model richer phrase composition than a standard recursive neural network.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because Recursive neural tensor network synthesizes earlier ideas into a higher-level mechanism or takeaway.

kp_catalog.name: Phrase-level sentiment supervision
kp_catalog.description: Labeling every phrase node in a parse tree creates richer supervision than sentence-level labels alone.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because Phrase-level sentiment supervision captures the core idea that Labeling every phrase node in a parse tree creates richer supervision than sentence-level labels alone.

kp_catalog.name: Negation and compositional sentiment
kp_catalog.description: Modeling how modifiers and negation change sentiment requires structure-sensitive composition, not just keyword counts.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because Negation and compositional sentiment synthesizes earlier ideas into a higher-level mechanism or takeaway.
